chunks.text: Laurent El Ghaoui is Professor Emeritus at UC Berkeley's EECS department. He graduated from Ecole Polytechnique in 1985 and earned a Ph.D. in Aeronautics and Astronautics from Stanford University in 1990. From 1992 to 1999, he was a faculty member at Ecole Nationale Supérieure de Techniques Avancées in Paris, with part-time teaching roles at Ecole Polytechnique and Université de Paris-I. In 1998, he received the CNRS Bronze Medal for Engineering Sciences. He joined UC Berkeley in 1999 as an Acting Associate Professor, gained tenure in 2001, and was on leave from 2003 to 2006 to work for SAC Capital Management.
chunks.question: What is Laurent El Ghaoui's academic background and career timeline?
chunks.answer: Ph.D. from Stanford, professor at UC Berkeley.

chunks.text: His research areas include Control, Intelligent Systems, and Robotics, focusing on robust and convex optimization, decision-making under uncertainty, statistical estimation, air traffic management, bioinformatics, and finance. He is affiliated with research centers such as the Berkeley Artificial Intelligence Research Lab, Berkeley Center for New Media, CLIMB, and Industrial Engineering and Operations Research.
chunks.question: What are Laurent El Ghaoui's main research interests?
chunks.answer: Robust optimization, decision-making, and AI applications.

chunks.text: Selected publications include works on stochastic optimization, sparse machine learning for text corpora, air traffic flow scheduling, Gaussian graphical models, chance-constrained linear programs, basket option pricing, microarray data classification, robust control of Markov processes, hyperplane classifiers, model predictive control, air traffic management algorithms, ellipsoidal bounds, portfolio optimization, classification approaches, aircraft routing, robust filtering, wireless sensor networks, MEMS design, and watermarking techniques.
chunks.question: What topics do Laurent El Ghaoui's publications cover?
chunks.answer: Optimization, machine learning, and control systems.

chunks.text: He has received honors including the SIAM Activity Group Optimization Prize in 2008, Okawa Research Grant in 2001, NSF CAREER Award in 2000, and CNRS Bronze Medal in 1998. His contact information includes office at 421 Sutardja Dai Hall, email elghaoui@eecs.berkeley.edu, and office hours on Wednesdays from 9:00-10:00am.
chunks.question: What awards has Laurent El Ghaoui won?
chunks.answer: SIAM prize, NSF award, and CNRS medal.
